do_not_merge.reason: Different legal entity names (Siemens Industry Inc. vs Siemens Mobility, Inc.) indicate distinct subsidiaries; per canon 'parent vs subsidiary never merge' (test 1: strong identity field entity_name differs).

weak_aliases.reason: 'Siemens' is an acronym/abbreviation used in source labels, but not a legal name.
'Siemens' is an acronym/abbreviation used in source labels, but not a legal name.

suggested_rules: When two entity records have explicit, distinct legal names (e.g., 'Siemens Industry Inc.' vs 'Siemens Mobility, Inc.') they are not the same canonical entity even if they share a common acronym or parent name; treat as separate subsidiaries.
suggestion.rationale: The two records have different entity names and descriptions, and the relation context involves separate projects. The shared acronym 'Siemens' is insufficient to merge distinct legal entities. Applying test 1 (contradiction) of the identity merge canon: entity_name differs, indicating they are different subsidiaries of the same parent, which per the canon are never merged.
